Perinatal Substance Use Prevention
Perinatal Substance Use Prevention
This Perinatal Substance Use Prevention Program offers education, training, and technical assistance for prenatal care providers in identifying women at risk for substance use, depression, and domestic violence. For those identified as high-risk, we provide comprehensive assessments, education and links to community resources.
Who is eligible?
This service is provided to prenatal care providers in San Luis Obispo County.
When and where is this service offered?
To learn more about the program or for technical assistance, please call (805) 781-5500 and ask to speak with the Program Coordinator. For more information on the program, visit
California’s Perinatal Substance Use Prevention website
.
Penny Borenstein, MD, MPH
Health Officer/Public Health Director
Director of Health Agency | Public Health: Penny Borenstein, MD, MPH, Health Officer/Public Health Director
